failedAssertions.reason: DataTable 'View in brain' button never rendered: condition checks `typeof value === 'string'` but API returns VBR data as object {min, max, q95, image, hash, center, idx}. Root cause: ResultsPanel DataTable handleViewInBrain uses base64 string directly but needs to extract value.image for object-format VBR data.

Blocked by VAL-BRAIN-002 bug (no 'View in brain' button to trigger overlay). Secondary bug: BrainViewer.syncOverlays uses Niivue methods setOverlayList/addOverlay that don't exist in Niivue 0.43.7 — correct API is nv.addVolume(nvImage). Color bar UI renders correctly for probabilistic overlays.
Blocked by VAL-BRAIN-002 bug: cannot add overlays via normal user flow. OverlayManager UI correctly tracks multiple overlay state when overlays are added via context.

Blocked by VAL-BRAIN-002 bug: cannot add overlays via normal user flow to then test removal. OverlayManager remove button and 'Clear all' button correctly update state when overlays are added via context.

rootCauses.description: DataTable condition for showing 'View in brain' button: `typeof cellValue === 'string' && cellValue.length > 0`. But the API returns VBR values as objects {min, max, q95, image, hash, center, idx} (not raw base64 strings). Fix: update condition to also handle object format, extract .image field for overlay loading.

syncOverlays() calls nv.setOverlayList() then falls back to nv.addOverlay() — neither method exists in Niivue 0.43.7. Exceptions silently swallowed, so no error is visible to user. Correct API: nv.addVolume(nvImage) to add overlays. To remove overlays: remove the volume from nv.volumes array and call nv.updateGLVolume().

appliedUpdates.description: Added brain-viz testing guidance: production build URL (port 3150), VBR query instructions, UI data-testid attributes for BrainViewer, OverlayManager, ColorBar. Added known bugs from brain-viz round 1.
Updated CodeMirror editor access pattern: use `.cm-content.cmTile.view` (not `.cm-editor.__cmView` which doesn't work). Also documented viewport requirement (1440x900) for brain viewer visibility.
